seoTitle: South Grafton vs South Granville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Grafton and South Granville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Grafton vs South Granville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Grafton and South Granville.
heading: South Grafton vs South Granville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Grafton, New South Wales and South Granville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Grafton has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to South Granville (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Grafton or South Granville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Grafton has a flood risk score of 44/100, while South Granville scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Grafton scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. South Granville scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Grafton or South Granville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Grafton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
